channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Hurricane Helene aftermath poses election hurdles in critical battlegrounds
item.description: Hurricane Helene has thrown up new hurdles for voters and election officials alike in Georgia and North Carolina, threatening disruptions to the voting process in two of the most critical battleground states.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Israel expands its bombardment in Lebanon as thousands flee widening war
item.description: MASNAA BORDER CROSSING, Lebanon (AP) — Powerful new explosions rocked Beirut's southern suburbs late Saturday as Israel expanded its bombardment in Lebanon, also striking a Palestinian refugee camp deep in the north for the first time as it targeted both Hezbollah and Hamas fighters.
item.dc:creator: BASSAM HATOUM, MELANIE LIDMAN and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump rallies at same Pennsylvania grounds where gunman tried to assassinate him
item.description: BUTLER, Pa. (AP) — Donald Trump picked up where he left off back in July when a gunman tried to assassinate him but only struck his ear before he raised his fist and shouted “Fight!” and was whisked away with blood across his face.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, JILL COLVIN and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Atlantic City hotel rate-fixing class action suit dismissed
item.description: A judge in Camden, New Jersey, ruled that the consumers had failed to present enough evidence to let their price-fixing lawsuit move ahead.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Former New York governor and stepson assaulted during evening walk
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former New York Gov. David Paterson and his stepson sustained minor injuries when they were assaulted on a Manhattan street by four men and a woman as they went for an evening walk, police said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tropical Storm Milton forms in the Gulf, NHC says
item.description: Tropical Depression Fourteen has strengthened into Tropical Storm Milton, according to the National Hurricane Center.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WGN 9

item.title: Live updates: Trump takes swipe at political opponents during Butler rally
item.description: Former President Trump is holding a rally on Saturday night rally in Butler, Pa., his first appearance in the town since a bullet grazed his ear at an event there nearly three months ago.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Where do Trump, Harris stand on marijuana policy?
item.description: In a poll taken last year, roughly 70% of American adults said marijuana should be legal — but where do the presidential candidates stand?
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Jersey sisters battle stage 4 colon cancer together
item.description: They share a lot more than their sister bond. They are both in their late 30s and currently battling stage four colon cancer.
item.dc:creator: Grace Gomez, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Many loathe public speakerphone calls, so why do people do it?
item.description: Few things are as universally reviled as a loud phone conversation infringing on your personal space. (Mosquitoes and taxes, aside.) Doing it on loudspeaker is even more incendiary.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: For small cities across Alabama with Haitian populations, Springfield is a cautionary tale
item.description: Many small Alabama cities have been beset by the same polarizing debate that thrust Springfield, Ohio, into the spotlight after former president Donald Trump promoted debunked misinformation about Haitian migrants.
item.dc:creator: Safiyah Riddle, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Homeowners hit by Hurricane Helene face the grim task of rebuilding without flood insurance
item.description: A week after Hurricane Helene overwhelmed the Southeastern U.S., homeowners hit the hardest are grappling with how they could possibly pay for the flood damage from one of the deadliest storms to hit the mainland in recent history.
item.dc:creator: SALLY HO, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Russian prosecutors seek 7-year sentence for US man accused of fighting for Ukraine
item.description: Russian prosecutors have asked for a seven-year sentence in the trial of a U.S. citizen accused of fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine against Russia, Russian news agencies reported Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Minnesota boy arrested after allegedly driving stolen car near playground where children were playing
item.description: The 10-year-old boy, who reportedly has a history of auto theft and threats of violence, was arrested in Minneapolis.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawyer for Diddy child accuser vows to out famous accomplices 'we all know'
item.description: The Texas lawyer who says he's representing more than dozens of potential victims of alleged sex trafficking music mogul Sean "Diddy" Combs vowed to name names in an ominous warning this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Texas execution is renewing calls for clemency. It's rarely granted
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A Texas man set to die this month is at the center of another push for clemency in the U.S., this time backed by several GOP lawmakers and bestselling author John Grisham, who say a father’s 2002 conviction for killing his infant daughter deserves a second look.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN and SUMMER BALLENTINE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bands drop out of Orlando music festival after Kyle Rittenhouse booked as guest
item.description: Shell Shock organizers describe the festival as a concert charity event that seeks to raise funds for a charity that "supports first responders struggling with injuries to include Post Traumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD).
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Dozens of illegal immigrants from 'special interest' nations with terrorist activity caught at southern border
item.description: Two large groups of illegal immigrants were caught this week at the Texas-Mexico border and had dozens from "special interest" countries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex- Virginia cop who killed shoplifting suspect acquitted of manslaughter, guilty on firearm charge
item.description: FAIRFAX, Va. (AP) — A jury on Friday acquitted a former police officer in Virginia of involuntary manslaughter after he fatally shot a shoplifting suspect outside a busy shopping mall.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BARAKAT and OLIVIA DIAZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: When will the power return? Weary Carolinas residents long for relief after Helene's fury
item.description: ASHEVILLE, N.C. (AP) — The weary and worn residents of Julianne Johnson's neighborhood in Asheville have been getting by without electricity since Hurricane Helene tore through the Southeast last week and upended their lives.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge denies an order sought by a Black student who was punished over his hair
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A federal judge on Friday denied a request by a Black high school student in Texas for a court order that the student’s lawyers say would have allowed him to return to his high school without fear of having his previous punishment over his hairstyle resume.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Why are there still so many blackouts after major storms?
item.description: The high costs of infrastructure improvements likely have contributed to the blackouts. Experts say more can be done to prevent power outages, but it would require significant investments.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Los Angeles mayor picks ex-sheriff to be city's police chief
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Former LA County Sheriff Jim McDonnell has been selected to lead the Los Angeles Police Department, taking charge of the force of nearly 9,000 officers as discontent grows among the city's residents over public safety even as violent crime numbers drop, the mayor announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New judge could give Diddy another attempt to win bail
item.description: While Diddy's legal team hasn’t commented, the assignment of a new judge potentially opens the door for a third bail hearing.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Some perplexed at jury's mixed verdict in trial for 3 former officers in Tyre Nichols' death
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Moments after a federal judge read the partial convictions in the federal trial of three former Memphis officers in the fatal beating of Tyre Nichols, an activist outside the courtroom could not contain his shock. “What the (expletive)?” Hunter Demster exclaimed to a reporter.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Obama to campaign for Harris in key swing states, starting with Pennsylvania
item.description: Obama’s first campaign stop for Harris will be in the Pittsburgh area on Oct. 10, according to a senior campaign official.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Search dogs trained in Southern California deployed for Hurricane Helene rescue efforts
item.description: Dogs that have been trained in Ventura County to respond to international disasters have been deployed to the Southern U.S. to aid in Hurricane Helene recovery efforts. Eleven canine disaster search teams from all over the country have traveled to the most heavily affected areas to lend a paw.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: McDonald's adding Chicken Big Mac to menus for a limited time
item.description: The sandwich is essentially built the same way as the Big Mac, albeit with two "tempura-battered chicken patties"
item.dc:creator: Nick Gladney, NewsNation

item.title: Springsteen calls Trump 'most dangerous candidate for president in my lifetime,' backs Harris
item.description: The rock legend said the former president “doesn’t understand the meaning of this country, its history or what it means to be deeply American.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: NYC Deputy Mayor Sheena Wright expected to resign: Sources
item.description: Wright joins a growing list of members of Mayor Eric Adams’ inner circle who have resigned. Banks will step down from his position on Oct. 16.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, NewsNation

item.title: Watch Live: Trump returns to site of assassination attempt for another rally
item.description: Former President Donald Trump is returning Saturday to the site of his attempted assassination with a rally in Butler County.
item.dc:creator: Patty Coller, Fox 5

item.title: Oklahoma defends Bibles-in-schools proposal after report that only Trump's might qualify
item.description: Oklahoma Education Superintendent Ryan Walters’s (R) office is defending the process for how the state will be selecting newly mandated Bibles in classrooms after a report was released that the criteria is so narrow that essentially no Bibles qualify — besides ones endorsed by former President Trump
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US launches airstrikes on Yemen’s Iran-backed Houthi rebels
item.description: Military aircraft and warships bombed Houthi strongholds at roughly five locations, according to the officials.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden: 'I don't know' if Netanyahu trying to influence election
item.description: President Biden on Friday said he wasn't aware of whether Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu was trying to influence the U.S. election, while noting no administration has been a stronger ally to Israel than his own.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: What renters should know about breaking a lease to buy a home
item.description: Renters can break their lease to purchase their own home but they should understand the ramifications of leaving early
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Giant bear claws out from under Southern California home
item.description: Bear break-ins in the San Gabriel foothill communities are common and most homeowners have learned to cope with the big, furry nuisances.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: Rosie O’Donnell: Knowing the Menendez brothers has been ‘healing’
item.description: "Back in 1989, no one was ready to believe that fathers raped their sons. But they do,” O’Donnell told NewsNation’s “CUOMO.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation
